scope: Google Ads REST is organized around custom service methods rather than conventional REST list/get/create/update/delete endpoints. This connector covers legacy's conservative read allow-list only: customer discovery plus campaign and ad_group GAQL search rows. campaigns/ad_groups are declared method GET here to express read semantics for api_surface validation even though the wire transport is POST /googleAds:search with a JSON GAQL body; Google Ads uses POST for both read search and write mutate methods, so the wire verb alone is not a capability boundary.

endpoints.path: /customers/{customerId}/googleAds:search (all other GAQL resources, reports, metrics, and segments)

excluded.reason: The legacy connector deliberately rejects arbitrary GAQL and the Tier-2 StreamHook has a closed allow-list for campaign and ad_group only; adding the full GAQL resource/reporting catalog would require hook/code changes and new record contracts outside this defs-only shard.

Official REST docs describe SearchStream as returning equivalent results for the same GAQL queries; legacy and this bundle cover the paginated search method instead because it preserves the existing page_size/max_pages behavior.

endpoints.path: /customers/{customerId}/googleAds:searchStream (all other GAQL resources, reports, metrics, and segments)

excluded.reason: Same arbitrary-GAQL exclusion as googleAds:search; SearchStream is a streaming transport for the same unbounded query surface and is not implemented by legacy.

endpoints.path: /googleAdsFields:search and other API metadata/search helper methods

excluded.reason: These introspection/helper methods are outside legacy's emitted record surface and would need additional hook support for POST-body GAQL-style reads.

Accessible customer discovery intentionally emits only customer_id and resource_name like legacy; full account hierarchy or customer metadata would be a new GAQL stream with a different schema and hook route.

Generic GoogleAdsService mutate accepts heterogeneous create/update/remove operations across many resource types; exposing it would be a broad generic write tool rather than a bounded reverse-ETL action, and legacy Write is unsupported.

Resource-specific mutate methods create, update, or remove live ads-account resources through operations arrays and update masks; each safe action would require a dedicated per-resource schema/risk review and is not present in legacy.
Async job creation, polling, and result retrieval require multi-step orchestration not expressible as a single declarative stream/write action in this bundle.

endpoints.path: /customers/{customerId}/conversionUploads:* and offline user data/customer match uploads

excluded.reason: Upload endpoints submit conversion or customer/user data and commonly require elevated account permissions or allowlisting; they are high-risk write/import workflows and are absent from legacy.
